message:
fcp-manifest: reject unbounded rate-limit pool declarations

Previously a manifest could declare a rate-limit pool with
`requests = u32::MAX, window_ms = 1` and pass validation — the host
would then interpret that as "up to 4 billion requests per ms",
which is indistinguishable from disabling the limiter entirely.
Similarly, `pools` / `operation_pools` had no cap, so a malicious or
buggy manifest could blow up memory with millions of entries.

Add explicit upper bounds enforced at validate time:

  - requests  <= 1_000_000_000
  - burst     <= 1_000_000_000
  - window_ms <=    86_400_000  (24h)
  - pools.len()                 <= 1024
  - operation_pools.len()       <= 4096
  - operation_pools[*].len()    <=   16

All bounds are far above any reasonable connector configuration
(existing tests use requests <= 100_000 and window_ms <= 60_000) but
prevent the "declared pool degenerates into unlimited" bypass and
cap memory at parse time.

[HIGH] Resource-limit bypass via large pools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
